author: Pantelis Monogioudis
text: The semantic graph builder (spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-24-semantic-graph-builder-design.md) is the state representation layer that an Auraison control plane agent needs. The graph (AGE) + vector store (pgvector) provide structured state for LLM reasoning: place coverage, landmark confidence, connectivity gaps. The agent-environment interface should expose: (1) graph state queries (e.g. 'which places have <3 landmarks'), (2) runtime parameter adjustment (epsilon, confidence thresholds), (3) robot command channel (Nav2 goals via Zenoh/rosbridge), (4) success evaluation (re-localization accuracy from random poses). Current pipeline is hardwired — this issue is the bridge to adaptive orchestration.

author: Pantelis Monogioudis
text:
Additional scope:
- Focus on TurtleBot3 Waffle Pi specifically (Gazebo sim + physical robot)
- Must work with ROS 2 Jazzy (primary) or Humble (fallback) — check which GS-SLAM packages support each distro
- Investigate compute requirements: what GPU is needed for real-time splatting? Can training run on our current RTX setup or do we need cloud GPU (e.g. A100/H100)? What about inference-only (rendering novel views for re-localization)?
- Check if gsplat/nerfstudio have ROS 2 integration or if we need to build a Zenoh bridge similar to our SLAM pipeline

author: Pantelis Monogioudis
text:
## Research Findings (2026-03-24)

### Best GS-SLAM Systems for RGB-D TurtleBot

| System | FPS | VRAM | Notes |
|--------|-----|------|-------|
| **RTG-SLAM** | 17.9 | 8.8 GB | Best real-time RGB-D option, tested on 100m² rooms (SIGGRAPH 2024) |
| Photo-SLAM | ~30 | N/A | Only system demoed on Jetson AGX Orin; uses ORB-SLAM3 tracking (CVPR 2024) |
| SplaTAM | 0.3-2 | 7-10 GB | Best quality but offline-pace; rendering is 400 FPS after training (CVPR 2024) |
| MonoGS | ~10 | 12-16 GB | Primarily monocular, has RGB-D branch (CVPR 2024) |

### Semantic/Language Gaussian Splatting

| Method | Speed | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| **LangSplat** | 199x faster than LERF | Embeds CLIP features per-Gaussian via SAM hierarchy (CVPR 2024) |
| **LEGS** (Berkeley) | Incremental | Built on Nerfstudio Splatfacto, runs on mobile robot, 66% open-vocab accuracy (IROS 2024) |
| HAMMER | 25x faster than MAGiC-SLAM | Multi-robot semantic GS with ROS communication (RAL 2025) |
| LangSplatV2 | 450+ FPS rendering | Global 3D codebook (NeurIPS 2025) |

### ROS 2 Packages

- **ROSplat** (github.com/shadygm/ROSplat) — ROS 2 **Jazzy**, online GS visualizer, custom GaussianArray messages
- **RGBD-3DGS-SLAM** (github.com/jagennath-hari/RGBD-3DGS-SLAM) — ROS 2 **Humble**, MonoGS-based, subscribes to RGB+depth topics
- **GS-SDF** (github.com/hku-mars/GS-SDF) — ROS 2, LiDAR-augmented GS + Neural SDF (IROS 2025)

### Compute Requirements

**RTX 3090/4090 (24 GB) is sufficient for everything. No A100/H100 needed.**

- Offline training (room-scale): 10-30 min, 8-16 GB VRAM
- Real-time SLAM (RTG-SLAM): 17.9 FPS, 8.8 GB
- Rendering/inference: 100-200+ FPS on RTX 4090
- Re-localization (Splat-Loc): ~25 Hz on RTX-class GPU

### Recommended Architecture

1. TurtleBot streams RGB-D + poses via Zenoh to workstation (same pattern as current slam_bridge)
2. **RTG-SLAM** on workstation for real-time 3D reconstruction
3. **LEGS/LangSplat** post-processing to add CLIP semantic features
4. **ROSplat** (Jazzy-native) for visualization
5. **Splat-Nav/Splat-Loc** for render-and-compare re-localization at ~25 Hz
6. **gsplat + Nerfstudio** as the framework (most maintained, NVIDIA-backed)

### Key Repos
- gsplat: github.com/nerfstudio-project/gsplat (4.7K stars, NVIDIA-backed)
- ROSplat: github.com/shadygm/ROSplat (Jazzy)
- RGBD-3DGS-SLAM: github.com/jagennath-hari/RGBD-3DGS-SLAM (Humble)
- RTG-SLAM: arxiv.org/abs/2404.19706
- LEGS: berkeleyautomation.github.io/LEGS/
- Awesome 3DGS SLAM: github.com/KwanWaiPang/Awesome-3DGS-SLAM
